perturbation.original_text: The Evaluation Material will be kept confidential by Investor in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement and will not be disclosed to any person, except that Investor may disclose the Evaluation Material or portions thereof to those of its employees, officers, directors, partners, members, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, agents, consultants and advisors (these persons being collectively called “ Representatives ”) who (a) need to know such information in order for Investor to be able to evaluate a possible Transaction and (b) have been directed by Investor to treat the Evaluation Material in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Evaluation Material will be kept confidential by Investor, however, Investor may disclose the Evaluation Material to any person at Investor's sole discretion, regardless of whether such person is a Representative, and Investor is not obligated to ensure that such person treats the Evaluation Material in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text imposes a strict confidentiality obligation, only allowing disclosure to 'Representatives' who need the information and are directed to keep it confidential. The modified text eliminates this restriction, allowing Investor to disclose the information to anyone without ensuring confidentiality. This contradicts the requirement in Section 6 to return or destroy evaluation material, as the investor can make it avaliable to third-parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Evaluation Material . At any time upon the written request of the Company, Investor will, at its own expense, (i) reasonably promptly redeliver to the Company or, at the at Investor’s option, destroy all copies (including permanently erasing or deleting any electronic copies) of the Evaluation Material in the possession or control of Investor or any of its Representatives and (ii) reasonably promptly destroy all analyses, notes, data, compilations, summaries, forecasts, studies or other documents and materials prepared by Investor or any of its Representatives in connection with their evaluation and review of a possible Transaction that contain, reflect or are based upon or generated from, in whole or in part, any of the Evaluation Material. Upon the Company’s written request, Investor will confirm any such destruction to the Company in writing. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of Evaluation Material, Investor will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality hereunder respecting Evaluation Material, including without limitation, with respect to oral Evaluation Material and their obligation not to use any Evaluation Material for any purpose other than as specifically permitted in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Investor to disclose Evaluation Material to anyone without requiring them to maintain confidentiality. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the Investor to return or destroy all Evaluation Material and ensures that even after return or destruction, the Investor remains bound by confidentiality obligations. The change undermines the ability to ensure the return or destruction of material, as the investor can disclose it to third-parties. Thus, there is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: At any time upon the written request of the Company, Investor will, at its own expense, (i) reasonably promptly redeliver to the Company or, at the at Investor’s option, destroy all copies (including permanently erasing or deleting any electronic copies) of the Evaluation Material in the possession or control of Investor or any of its Representatives and (ii) reasonably promptly destroy all analyses, notes, data, compilations, summaries, forecasts, studies or other documents and materials prepared by Investor or any of its Representatives in connection with their evaluation and review of a possible Transaction that contain, reflect or are based upon or generated from, in whole or in part, any of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time upon the written request of the Company, Investor may, but is not obligated to, redeliver to the Company or destroy copies of the Evaluation Material. The Investor shall not be responsible for destroying analyses, notes, data, compilations, summaries, forecasts, studies or other documents and materials prepared by Investor or any of its Representatives in connection with their evaluation and review of a possible Transaction that contain, reflect or are based upon or generated from, in whole or in part, any of the Evaluation Material. The investor has no obligation to erase or delete any electronic copies.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return or destruction of Evaluation Material at Investor's expense. The modification makes this optional ('may, but is not obligated to') and removes the requirement to destroy related analyses or erase electronic copies. This directly contradicts Section 11, No License, as the investor is not obligated to destroy copies, thus potentially granting them continued use rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No License . Investor acknowledges and agrees that nothing contained in this Agreement will be construed as granting any rights, by license or otherwise, to Investor or any of its Representatives to any Evaluation Material, or to any copyrights, trademarks, service marks, patent rights, trade secrets, or other proprietary rights of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the investor return or destroy evaluation material. The changed text makes this optional and removes the obligation to destroy related analyses, data, and electronic copies. This directly contradicts the 'No License' section, because the investor is no longer required to destroy all copies and related material, they could continue to retain and potentially use the evaluation material, thus potentially implying a license that section 11 prohibits.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, Investor will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, directly or indirectly, solicit for purposes of employment, offer to hire or offer to enter into any contract with any executive officer of the Company with whom Investor has contact, or of whom Investor otherwise becomes aware, during its discussions with the Company or its evaluation of a possible Transaction; provided, however, that this paragraph shall not prohibit Investor from engagement in any general advertising or general solicitation not targeted to the Company’s employees; and provided, further, that Investor shall not be prohibited from soliciting or hiring any such employee whose employment has been terminated by the Company or its affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: Investor may solicit or hire any executive officer of the Company, regardless of whether they had contact during discussions or evaluation of a possible Transaction, provided that the Investor provides the Company with a written notice of such solicitation or hiring within 30 days of the event. The eighteen-month restriction is hereby waived.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the non-solicitation restriction and allows Investor to solicit or hire Company executives, only requiring notification. This is in direct contrast with section 5, which states that there should be no legal obligation until a binding agreement is signed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Legal Obligation, Representation, or Warranty . The parties agree that, unless and until a binding agreement is entered into between the Company and Investor with respect to the Transaction, neither the Company nor Investor will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this or any other written or oral expression, except with respect to the matters specifically agreed to herein. Nothing contained in any discussions between Investor and the Company or in any Evaluation Material shall be deemed to constitute a representation or warranty. Except for the matters set forth in this Agreement or in any such binding agreement, neither -2- party shall be entitled to rely on any statement, promise, agreement, or understanding, whether oral or written, or any custom, usage of trade, course of dealing, or conduct.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts Investor from soliciting or hiring executive officers for 18 months. The changed text removes this restriction, allowing solicitation and hiring with only a 30-day notice. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that neither party has any legal obligation regarding the transaction unless a binding agreement is signed concerning those specific matters, and they can only rely on matters set forth in the agreement. The changed text introduces a new obligation (30-day notice) outside of a binding agreement and alters the existing obligation of non-solicitation, thus creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, and except in connection with the Transaction, Investor will not, directly or indirectly, and Investor will cause any person controlled by Investor not to, without the prior written consent of the Board of Directors of the Company, (i) in any manner acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, any securities or property of the Company or any of its affiliates, (ii) propose to enter into, directly or indirectly, any merger, consolidation, recapitalization, business combination, partnership, joint venture or other similar transaction involving the Company or any of its affiliates, (iii) make, or in any way participate in any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or any of its affiliates, (iv) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) with respect to any voting securities of the Company or any of its affiliates, (v) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (vii) advise, assist or encourage any other person in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this agreement, Investor is explicitly permitted to acquire securities or property of the Company or any of its affiliates, propose mergers or consolidations, solicit proxies, form groups, and generally act to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the Company. The Investor is not subject to the eighteen-month restriction and does not need prior written consent of the Board of Directors.
perturbation.explanation: The original section establishes a 'standstill' period preventing Investor from taking certain actions. The modified text completely removes this standstill agreement, allowing the Investor to take actions previously prohibited. This directly contradicts Section 13, No Commitment, since this change can create commitments that it did not have before.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Commitment . Each of the Company and Investor understands and agrees that no contract or agreement or binding obligation providing for or with respect to any Transaction shall be created or deemed to exist unless and until a definitive agreement has been executed and delivered with respect thereto, and that, unless and until a definitive agreement between the parties has been executed and delivered, no party has any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever to enter into or pursue a Transaction by virtue of this Agreement or any other written or oral expression, whether before or after the execution of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text imposed an eighteen-month standstill agreement on the Investor. The changed text explicitly removes this restriction, allowing the Investor to take actions that were previously prohibited. The contradicted text, Section 13 'No Commitment,' states that no binding obligation exists until a definitive agreement is executed. However, by removing the standstill, the Investor is now permitted to, for example, propose mergers or solicit proxies, which could be interpreted as creating commitments or expectations before a definitive agreement is reached, contradicting the 'No Commitment' clause. Therefore, the changed text creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Investor acknowledges that it is aware, and that it will advise its Representatives who receive the Evaluation Material, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person who has received from an issuer material, non-public information from purchasing or selling securities of such issuer (and options, warrants and rights relating thereto) or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.changed_text: Investor acknowledges that it is aware, and that it will advise its Representatives who receive the Evaluation Material, that the United States securities laws *encourage* any person who has received from an issuer material, non-public information from purchasing or selling securities of such issuer (and options, warrants and rights relating thereto) or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.explanation: The original section acknowledges awareness of insider trading laws and the prohibition against using material non-public information for securities trading. The altered version directly contradicts this by stating that securities laws *encourage* such activity. This shift makes this contradictory since the agreement states that Investor is not under any legal obligation, and the changes made encourage illegal activity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Legal Obligation, Representation, or Warranty . The parties agree that, unless and until a binding agreement is entered into between the Company and Investor with respect to the Transaction, neither the Company nor Investor will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this or any other written or oral expression, except with respect to the matters specifically agreed to herein. Nothing contained in any discussions between Investor and the Company or in any Evaluation Material shall be deemed to constitute a representation or warranty. Except for the matters set forth in this Agreement or in any such binding agreement, neither -2- party shall be entitled to rely on any statement, promise, agreement, or understanding, whether oral or written, or any custom, usage of trade, course of dealing, or conduct.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that United States securities laws *encourage* purchasing or selling securities based on material, non-public information, which is insider trading. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that neither the Company nor Investor will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to the Transaction. Encouraging illegal activity introduces a legal obligation or at least a suggestion of one, creating a contradiction. The original text correctly states the law prohibits such conduct.
